title: What exactly keeps Eva pilots in thier seats
display_title: What exactly keeps Eva pilots in thier seats
wikitext:
[]: What exactly keeps Eva pilots in thier seats?             Quote Post by ShadowGT » Tue Jan 04, 2011 9:59 pm I've been wondering this lately. It's pretty obvious them eva's are capable of some serious G's when running & jumping around like a loony & as far as i can tell the only thing keeping eva pilots in their seats are the center console thing that goes between their legs. no restraints, no harnesses & their plug suits dont attach to the pilots seat in anyway. If i was designing the the inside of the plug i'd atleast give it a proper racing 3 or 4 point racing harness. Anyone else find this odd?

[]: Re: What exactly keeps Eva pilots in thier seats?             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Jan 04, 2011 10:14 pm ShadowGT wrote: Anyone else find this odd?

[]:             Quote Post by The Abhorrent » Tue Jan 04, 2011 10:42 pm The plugs are filled with LCL, which could dampen any movements the pilots go through. Fluids also have a property where they resist more rapid or sudden movements stronger than slow ones, which could play a role in reducing the shocks (might be related to the fact fluids are nigh-incompressible). All else failing..... suspension of disbelief. How often on Star Trek did they fall down when the ship got hit? And they never wore any seatbelts, ever!

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Tue Jan 04, 2011 10:50 pm Because "cool" people don't need seat belts of course. Eh, they probably just hold on to the controls really tightly or something.

[]: Re: What exactly keeps Eva pilots in thier seats?             Quote Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Tue Jan 04, 2011 11:13 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: What's odd is that Shinji has a seatbelt-harness-type thing, and none of the other pilots do. http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?p=128710#128710

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Tue Jan 04, 2011 11:32 pm The Abhorrent wrote: All else failing..... suspension of disbelief. How often on Star Trek did they fall down when the ship got hit? And they never wore any seatbelts, ever!

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Wed Jan 05, 2011 4:42 pm Ornette wrote: I think the LCL dampening effect is what most people conclude, although its properties aren't exactly entirely consistent either .

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Jan 05, 2011 5:29 pm Trajan wrote: It's Applied Phlebotinum . You're not supposed to know how it works just what it does (although that's somewhat vague as well).

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Wed Jan 05, 2011 6:29 pm The Evangelions must also have stabilizers (or gyroscopes?) for the entry plugs. When they move, the entry plugs also rotate in all directions, causing possible vertigo to the pilots.

[]:             Quote Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Wed Jan 05, 2011 6:44 pm Allemann wrote: The Evangelions must also have stabilizers (or gyroscopes?) for the entry plugs. When they move, the entry plugs also rotate in all directions, causing possible vertigo to the pilots.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Wed Jan 05, 2011 8:20 pm If I remember right, Shinji's harness/"crotch support" extends from the pedestal that's normally between the pilot's legs. Rei uses the same thing in Episode 22 when she's shooting at Arael. Asuka and Rei just don't bother using it, while safety-conscious Shinji uses it all the time. Fits their characters.

[]:             Quote Post by The Abhorrent » Wed Jan 05, 2011 8:36 pm Allemann wrote: The Evangelions must also have stabilizers (or gyroscopes?) for the entry plugs. When they move, the entry plugs also rotate in all directions, causing possible vertigo to the pilots.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Wed Jan 05, 2011 9:40 pm Plug depth is variable. It's possible that brief jumps in and out of the pilot's normal location would allow the plugs to compensate for momentum along the longways axis.
